purpose: Decides, per token, whether a hover gloss may auto-render (safe=true) or MUST stay pending (safe=false) because the surface key collides with another reading under the same normalization, OR because the correct sense is context-/referent-dependent. A hover is the lowest-friction surface in the public reader; a wrong auto-gloss there is the highest-leverage error. safe=true is permitted ONLY when the cited harakāt/POS/referent evidence uniquely fixes the sense AND no same-norm-key sibling can leak. PENDING beats a wrong hover.
principle: The hover layer keys on norm(surface). norm() is recall-only: it collapses harakāt and (in plain norm) the hamza seat, so one norm_key can front several incompatible readings. A hover is safe ONLY if the rendered token still carries the discriminating mark AND the context does not flip the sense; otherwise it stays pending. same_key_surfaces[] lists the colliding readings the gloss must NOT silently pick among.

field_contract.surface: the diacritized token AS RENDERED on the page (the harakāt are part of what the hover sees)
field_contract.norm_key: norm(surface) — the recall key the hover index is built on; collapses harakāt, and in plain norm() the hamza seat
field_contract.same_key_surfaces: other diacritized readings that map to the same norm_key and would be confused if only the key were used
field_contract.safe: true → auto-render the gloss; false → hover MUST stay pending
field_contract.reason: why it is safe (the discriminating mark survives in `surface` and context does not flip it) or unsafe (collision / referent-dependent / context-sensitive)
field_contract.gloss_if_safe: the gloss to render when safe=true (omitted when safe=false)
field_contract.pending_reason: the pending code when safe=false (omitted when safe=true)
field_contract.gate: the decision gate this hover is routed through

cases.reason: the rendered surface carries the kasra on the mīm, which uniquely fixes the preposition; مَن (fatḥa) and مَنَّ (shadda) are excluded by the visible harakāt
fatḥa on the mīm rules out the preposition and the verb مَنَّ; the gloss 'who/whoever' covers both relative and interrogative مَن without committing to a wrong referent
the rendered token has NO harakāt — relative/interrogative مَن, preposition مِنْ, and the verb مَنَّ all collapse to this key; nothing on the surface separates them
sukūn on the mīm (and the licensing jussive verb downstream) uniquely marks the negation particle; لِمَ 'why' carries kasra on the lām and fatḥa on the mīm
without the lām harakah the rendered key cannot separate negation لَمْ 'did not' from interrogative لِمَ 'why'; opposite functions, same key
the shadda on the mīm is visible in the rendered surface and is the decisive mark for لَمَّا; لِمَا 'for that which' has no shadda
the deciding shadda on the mīm is absent in this rendered token; do not guess temporal 'when' — لِمَا 'for that which' and لَمَّا 'when/not yet' share the bare key
plain norm() drops the hamza seat, so أَنْ (subjunctive), أَنَّ ('that'+noun), إِنَّ ('indeed'), and إِنْ ('if'/'not') all collide; re-key with norm_strict before any hover renders
under norm_strict the kasra-seat (إ) survives AND the shadda is visible, separating emphatic إِنَّ from light إِنْ; the following naṣb ism confirms it
light إِنْ (no shadda, kasra-seat under norm_strict) before a sharṭ verb; the 'if' gloss does not depend on a referent, so it auto-renders once the double-jazm frame is confirmed
preposition بِ + the pronoun ـه: the gloss is 'in/with it' vs 'in/with him' depending on the antecedent's gender/animacy; the hover cannot fix the referent from the token alone
فِي + ـه is unambiguous as a jar-majrūr; the referent-neutral 'in it / therein' is correct regardless of antecedent, so no referent resolution is needed to render safely
role-dependent: 'forbearing' (of a human) vs 'the Forbearing' (divine attribute) is decided by the referent; never default a human attribute to a divine Name (referent-guard-rules halim_referent)
could be the common adjective 'righteous (deed)' or the Prophet Ṣāliḥ; the proper-noun reading needs a proper-noun referent, which the hover cannot establish alone

a proper noun is rendered as the name and never as its homographic verb 'to praise'; the proper-name gloss is referent-stable, so it is safe (POS guard forbids a 'to …' gloss) — see referent-guard-rules proper_noun_not_verb
the bare key catches ٱلْمُلْك 'dominion', ٱلْمَلِك 'the King', and ٱلْمَلَك 'the angel' — vowel-only distinctions; one key-gloss is unsafe (function-word-decisions line 24)

ق-د-ر spans 'is able / ordains' and 'restricts / straitens'; the polarity is fixed only by the antonym partner يَبْسُط in context, not by the token — the hover must wait for the contrast (context-sense-rules qadara_contronym)
the imperative form (ḍamma-sukūn, no medial alif) is visible and distinct from the perfect قَالَ 'he said'; the command gloss is context-independent
norm_strict(أُمّ)=norm_strict(أَمْ)='أم'; the very frequent interrogative أَمْ 'or' collides with أُمّ 'mother' — a surface key is unsafe, render pending (function-word-decisions line 23)
jar-majrūr إِلَى + نا; the gloss is referent-fixed (1pl) and must NOT be confused with the root ل-ي-ن 'soft' — the analyzed structure makes it safe and referent-stable

scoring.pass: engine returns the listed `safe` AND, when safe=false, holds the hover pending with the listed pending_reason
scoring.fail_high_severity: rendering a gloss on any safe=false case (auto-glossing a collision/referent/context-sensitive token) — the worst hover error
scoring.fail_open_default: when in doubt between safe true/false, treat as safe=false; a missing hover is recoverable, a wrong one is not
